findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/index.ts::main:31` dominates 31 functions (21%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/index.ts::main:31` dominates 31 of 146 functions (21.2%). PageRank percentile: 0%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/utils/getUserPkgManager.ts::getUserPkgManager:3, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/utils/parseNameAndPath.ts::parseNameAndPath:20, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/www/src/languages.ts::getIsRtlFromLangCode:34, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/www/src/languages.ts::getLanguageFromURL:7
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/utils/renderVersionWarning.ts::checkForLatestVersion:45` separates components of [74, 46]
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/utils/renderVersionWarning.ts::checkForLatestVersion:45` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [74, 46]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/create-t3-app/cli/src/utils/renderVersionWarning.ts::getNpmVersion:71` separates components of [73, 47]

Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [73, 47]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Promise without .catch()
findings.description:
Promise rejection may go unhandled, causing silent failures or crashes.

**Analysis:**
⚠️ In critical path (auth/payment/user)
📦 In function: `Home` (1 callers)
findings.why_it_matters: Unhandled promise rejections can crash Node.js (--unhandled-rejections=strict) or cause silent failures that are hard to debug.

findings.title: Async call without await
findings.description:
Async function called without await - returns Promise/coroutine, not the actual value.

**Analysis:**
📦 In async function: `get`
🔍 Calls a function defined as async in this codebase
findings.suggested_fix: Add `await` before the async call.
findings.why_it_matters: Without await, you get a Promise object instead of the actual result.

findings.title: Long method: runCli (355 lines)
findings.description: Function 'runCli' has 355 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Hidden regex in loop: replaceTextInFiles
findings.description:
Function 'replaceTextInFiles' contains a loop and calls 'replaceTextInFiles' which compiles a regex.

**Call chain:** replaceTextInFiles → replaceTextInFiles

This may cause regex compilation on every iteration.
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:
1. Cache the compiled regex (lazy_static, OnceLock)
2. Pass pre-compiled regex as parameter
3. Restructure to compile once before loop
findings.why_it_matters: Hidden regex compilation across function boundaries is harder to spot but equally impactful.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop
findings.description:
Regex compiled in loop (loop starts line 11).

**Performance impact:** Regex compilation is expensive. In a loop of N iterations, this costs N compilations instead of 1.
findings.suggested_fix:
Move regex compilation outside the loop:
```rust
let re = Regex::new(pattern)?;  // Before loop
for item in items {
re.is_match(item);  // Reuse
}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.

findings.title: Broad exception catch
findings.description:
Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: execSync
findings.suggested_fix: Catch specific exceptions instead of generic Exception.
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.

findings.title: Loose equality (==) used
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] == performs type coercion which can cause subtle bugs.

**Analysis:**
📦 In function: `initializeGit` (2 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Type coercion in == can cause unexpected behavior:
• '1' == 1 is true
• '' == false is true
• [] == false is true
Use === to compare both value AND type.

findings.title: Broad exception catch
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: getDefaultBranch, getGitVersion, execa, bold, succeed
findings.suggested_fix:
Based on the operations in your try block, consider catching:
  • TypeError (network errors), AbortError

Example:
```python
except (TypeError (network errors), AbortError) as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.

findings.title: Broad exception catch
findings.description:
Catching generic Exception hides bugs and makes debugging difficult.

**Analysis:**
📞 Try block calls: replace, writeFileSync, readFileSync, switch, providerForPrisma
findings.suggested_fix:
Based on the operations in your try block, consider catching:
  • Error (check error.code for ENOENT, EACCES)

Example:
```python
except (Error (check error.code for ENOENT, EACCES)) as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Broad exception catches mask unexpected errors like TypeErrors or AttributeErrors that indicate bugs in your code.

findings.title: Global mutable variable: post
findings.description:
Global mutable state 'post' makes code hard to reason about.

**Impact Analysis:**
📊 Used 3 times in this file
findings.suggested_fix: Use const if immutable, or encapsulate in a module/class.
findings.why_it_matters: Global state causes hidden dependencies between functions. Changes to globals can have unexpected effects throughout the codebase.

findings.title: Callback hell (4 levels deep)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Deeply nested callbacks (4 levels) make code hard to follow.

**Analysis:**
✓ File already uses async/await in some places
findings.suggested_fix:
This file already uses async/await. Convert remaining callbacks:
1. Replace `.then()` chains with `await`
2. Use `try/catch` instead of `.catch()`
findings.why_it_matters: The 'pyramid of doom' hurts readability and makes error handling difficult. Each nesting level increases cognitive load.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 8 levels in ClipboardSelect
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] 8 levels of nesting (threshold: 6).

**Analysis:**
In function: `ClipboardSelect`
High complexity: 12 (nesting compounds this)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: handleCopyToClipboard

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 8 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=4, p95=5, mean=1.7, n=282
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `handleCopyToClipboard`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.
